business_ideas.idea: Co-working space network licensing MFM name to existing spaces
Hot-desk co-working in restaurants/retail spaces (Spacious model)
Niche co-working with shared interests (fitness, women, minorities)
Ice barrel recovery product for post-workout icing
Premium home sauna sponsorship and placement strategy
Sensory deprivation helmet for quiet/meditation experiences
Water-filled medicine balls for dynamic resistance training
Premium athletic braces and injury prevention gear
Designer walking boots and post-surgery recovery equipment
Luxury wheelchair designs with modern features

predictions.text: Two or three multi-billion dollar co-working companies will exist in 10 years
Recovery and wellness products will continue exploding in popularity like Yeti did

disagreements: Shaan is skeptical that hot-desk co-working is materially better than traditional WeWork model (Breather example); Sam argues niche/shared-interest positioning solves WeWork's stickiness problem.
dynamics: Sam drives enthusiasm with detailed product stories (Kobe/Hyperice, Ice Barrel); Shaan brings skepticism but gets convinced through frameworking. Both discuss Adam Newman's cult-of-personality leadership style as both intoxicating and dangerous. Energy is collaborative problem-solving.
frameworks: Short-term liability vs long-term liability: Traditional co-working signs 10yr leases but rents short-term, causing churn
Athlete-as-marketing: Use high-profile athlete adoption as native placement for unbranded locker-room products
Unbranded→Branded opportunity: Find unglamorous athlete recovery rituals, sell premium versions
LTV arbitrage for co-working: Tech/bank co-working spaces break even but recruit high-LTV customers
learnings: Shared interests are the stickiness lever for co-working: people stay for community, not desk
Athletes are most powerful marketing vehicles in consumer goods (Gatorade, Nike, Beats)
Recovery/wellness is untapped branded space vs performance; every pro uses unbranded ice, tape, braces
Expensive products signal quality/seriousness in health ($1,200 Ice Barrel vs $500 trash-can ice bath)
Self-belief and irrationality are leadership superpowers but double-edged (Adam Newman, Conor McGregor)
Dream-exercise onboarding (asking 'who do you want to become') builds loyalty and reveals what motivates people

banger_quotes.quote: We could build a business that makes $1 billion a year in revenue
It's a sword that cuts both ways—all extreme strengths come with corresponding evil
Doubt doubt if you're gonna doubt anything, doubt doubt

numbers_dropped: Spacious: 100-300 seats per location, 15 check-ins/month = ~$90k/month, 40% profit margin
Hyperice: $100M revenue last year, projected $200M this year, $700M valuation
Ice Barrel: $1,200-$2,000 per unit; if 40/day sold = $2.4M/month revenue
Game Ready: $35M annual revenue, acquired for $65M
Beats by Dre: sold to Apple for $3 billion
notable: Episode pivots from co-working skepticism to enthusiasm by introducing athlete-marketing-for-recovery framework; Preston Pezik's willingness to reinvest in co-working became credibility signal that shifted Shaan's view.
